title: Anything to gain from watching Death and Rebirth
display_title: Anything to gain from watching Death and Rebirth
wikitext:
[]: Anything to gain from watching Death and Rebirth?             Quote Post by Number 44 » Tue Feb 02, 2010 5:02 pm Been a casual Eva fan for some time, but just now getting around to collecting the series, and dissecting episode details. I watched the original DVD release of the series, and now own Platinum Collection, and End of Evangelion. Now, my question is do I have anything to gain from watching/buying Death and Rebirth? First half, as I understand, is a clip show of the series with the directors scenes added to tie the series in better to EoE. Second half being Air, the first half of EoE. But are there any differences? Anything I don't already have with my current Platinum Collection / EoE combo?

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Tue Feb 02, 2010 5:03 pm nah, not really if you already own platinum subs

[]: Re: Anything to gain from watching Death and Rebirth?             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Tue Feb 02, 2010 5:15 pm Number 44 wrote: But are there any differences?

[]:             Quote Post by IrkenEvangelion » Tue Feb 02, 2010 5:28 pm You'll miss them gathering to play Patchable's Kanon!! I LOVED that, my favorite classical song by far. Especially that composure.

[]:             Quote Post by Merridian » Tue Feb 02, 2010 5:33 pm Death is awesome. It's often dismissed as 'just a clipshow', but it really makes very little sense to someone who hasn't already seen the show. Its editing allows it to serve as the perfect 'prelude' to EoE—particularly how it highlights the Instrumentality/hedgehog dilemma aspect of the show in preparation for the meat of EoE. I'd give it a shot if I were you. Rebirth is pointless, though.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue Feb 02, 2010 5:41 pm Totally worth it just for the diatribe on Asuka from Toji and Kensuke.

[]:             Quote Post by ran1 » Tue Feb 02, 2010 6:20 pm NemZ wrote: Totally worth it just for the diatribe on Asuka from Toji and Kensuke.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Feb 02, 2010 6:50 pm Merridian wrote: Death is awesome. It's often dismissed as 'just a clipshow', but it really makes very little sense to someone who hasn't already seen the show. Its editing allows it to serve as the perfect 'prelude' to EoE

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Tue Feb 02, 2010 8:45 pm It’s defiantly worth seeing. The ending alone makes it worth watching. SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Tue Feb 02, 2010 8:50 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: Merridian wrote: Death is awesome. It's often dismissed as 'just a clipshow', but it really makes very little sense to someone who hasn't already seen the show. Its editing allows it to serve as the perfect 'prelude' to EoE

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Tue Feb 02, 2010 8:53 pm child of Lilith wrote: It’s defiantly worth seeing. The ending alone makes it worth watching. SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Merridian » Tue Feb 02, 2010 9:43 pm tomrule123 wrote: That would take a while... now that Manga lost the rights, who will continue the distribution? The only source I have (AND I WILL NOT GIVE IT TO ANYONE) is an MKV copy I found online, which contained the Renewal Quality.

[]:             Quote Post by schismatics » Tue Feb 02, 2010 11:39 pm Merridian wrote: tomrule123 wrote: That would take a while... now that Manga lost the rights, who will continue the distribution? The only source I have (AND I WILL NOT GIVE IT TO ANYONE) is an MKV copy I found online, which contained the Renewal Quality.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Wed Feb 03, 2010 1:12 am Death reevaluates the character a little bit. Yes, it uses clips from the Platinum series. But the arrangement and musical re-scoring underneath help the audience realize the scenes from a different character's perspective. The the source music in the scene where Asuka sleep-walks into Shinji's room is changed from Shinji's tape player from the series, to Asuka's violin being played in the tape player instead. With that violin playing it's almost as if it's her fantasy that Shinji fantasizes about her, and the whole scene takes on a different perspective. There are other examples of it throughout the film, using different means of techniques to probe the minds of the characters. Rebirth is just the first half of The End of Evangelion with different end credits song. But seeing as how Death is only currently available as part of the Death and Rebirth DVD presented by MANGA Entertainment, it's just something you're gonna have to deal with.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Wed Feb 03, 2010 3:48 am Xard wrote: nah, not really if you already own platinum DVDs

[]:             Quote Post by toe mash » Wed Feb 03, 2010 9:07 am IrkenEvangelion wrote: You'll miss them gathering to play Patchable's Kanon!! I LOVED that, my favorite classical song by far. Especially that composure.

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Wed Feb 03, 2010 12:59 pm Merridian wrote: tomrule123 wrote: That would take a while... now that Manga lost the rights, who will continue the distribution? The only source I have (AND I WILL NOT GIVE IT TO ANYONE) is an MKV copy I found online, which contained the Renewal Quality.

[]:             Quote Post by toe mash » Wed Feb 03, 2010 1:31 pm Yeah, I think theres a chance we can get the raw version :p

[]: New to the series. Question about Death and Rebirth             Quote Post by AsukaStrikes » Sat Jul 21, 2012 4:25 pm Is the Death and Rebirth movie worth watching? I am under the impression that the director's cut episode fills in all of the new content that is in the movie. Thus, should I just move on to The End of Evangelion movie after watching the director's cut episodes?

[]:             Quote Post by Eliaskar » Sat Jul 21, 2012 4:29 pm I personally don't think that you need to watch Death and Rebirth really, since it's just a recap really. After the original series you should probably just move on to End of Evangelion.
summary: []: Anything to gain from watching Death and Rebirth?             Quote Post by Number 44 » Tue Feb 02, 2010 5:02 pm Been a casual Eva fan for some time, but just now getting around to collecting the series, and dissecting episode details. I watched the original DVD release of the series, and now own Platinum Collection, and End of Evangelion. Now, my question is do I have anything to gain from watching/buying Death and Rebirth?
